minimal_context: The student is seated near the teacher's table and is expected to listen quietly to the lesson instructions.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please sit at your desk and listen quietly while I give the instructions for today's lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Fidgeting with a pencil and making tapping noises on the desk) Why do we have to do this? What are we doing next?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I will explain if you just listen.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shaking the desk and reaching over to take a classmate's eraser) Look at this eraser!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Put that down and focus on the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands up and starts walking around the classroom, interrupting the teacher's instruction) I'm bored, I want to see what's over there!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please sit at your desk and listen quietly while I give the instructions for today's lesson.

STUDENT: (Fidgeting with a pencil and making tapping noises on the desk) Why do we have to do this? What are we doing next?

TUTOR: I will explain if you just listen.

STUDENT: (Shaking the desk and reaching over to take a classmate's eraser) Look at this eraser!

TUTOR: Put that down and focus on the lesson.

STUDENT: (Stands up and starts walking around the classroom, interrupting the teacher's instruction) I'm bored, I want to see what's over there!

context.environment: regular classroom, closer to teacher’s table
context.social_setting: working with large numbers of students

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: passive classroom activities (e.g. listening to teacher instruction)
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: engaging in arguments/conversations with peers while the teacher was giving instructions, answering out of turn, making irrelevant comments, shaking desk or chair
related_quotes: Hyperactive students are more curious, they can never sit in their place, always mobile- but I don’t find anything wrong in that, they are active, ask a lot of questions ...

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: walking around the classroom, talking out of turn, intrusive verbalizations, interrupting teacher instruction
distracting students by taking away their stationery items, making noises
